zh.lifeSummary: 當Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach於大約1226年出生時，他的父親Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg43歲，母親Adelheid von Neuffen29歲。他於1248年5月18日與Sophia von Hohenzollern在德國海因里希_霍恩左勒結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他於1236年被列為regentschaft als graf von freiburg。他去世於1271年9月24日享年46歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach于大约1226年出生时，他的父亲Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg43岁，母亲Adelheid von Neuffen29岁。他于1248年5月18日与Sophia von Hohenzollern在德国海因里希_霍恩左勒结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他于1236年被列为regentschaft als graf von freiburg。他去世于1271年9月24日享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Freiburg,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places called Freiberg, from Middle High German vrī ‘free, independent’ + berg ‘mountain, hill’ (possibly an altered form of burg ‘castle, fortified town’).

variants.name: Frieberg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars.
Danish, Swedish, and North German: variant of German   Freiberg  .

variants.name: Freiberger
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Gerhard, Inge, Rudi.
German: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Freiberg (see   Freiberg  ).

variants.name: Friberg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Birgit, Elof, Lars, Nils, Per, Viljo.
Scandinavian: either an ornamental name composed of fri ‘free’ + berg ‘mountain, hill’ or a habitational name from a place so named.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Freiberg  .

variants.name: Freeberg
meanings: Americanized form of German Freiberg or Freiburg (see Freiburger ).

variants.name: Freeburg
meanings: Americanized form of German Freiburg or Freiberg .
Americanized form of Vreeburg, shortened variant of Vredenburg .

meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hoan, Duc, Hung, Thanh.  Jewish Reuven.

Americanized form of Jewish and Polish   Tracz  , a cognate of 1 above.

meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Dariusz, Zdzislaw, Bronislaus, Ignatius, Jozef, Zosia.

nameMeanings.name: Konrad
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from kuon ‘bold’ + rad ‘counsel’.

en.lifeSummary: When Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach was born about 1226, his father, Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg, was 43 and his mother, Adelheid von Neuffen, was 29. He married Sophia von Hohenzollern on 18 May 1248, in Hohenzollern, Prussia, Germany. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. In 1236, his occupation is listed as regentschaft als graf von freiburg. He died on 24 September 1271, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach est né environ 1226, son père, Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Adelheid von Neuffen, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Sophia von Hohenzollern le 18 mai 1248, à Hohenzollern, Prusse, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. En 1236, sa profession de son indiquée est regentschaft als graf von freiburg. Il est décédé le 24 septembre 1271, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendin: Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden, Deutschland
de.lifeSummary: Als Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach um 1226 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg 43 und seine Mutter Adelheid von Neuffen 29. Er hat am 18. Mai 1248 in Hohenzollern, Preußen, Deutschland Sophia von Hohenzollern geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. 1236 ist sein Beruf als regentschaft als graf von freiburg aufgeführt. Er starb am 24. September 1271 im Alter von 46 Jahren.

Graf von Freiburg, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Adelheid von Neuffen, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Sophia von Hohenzollern il 18 maggio 1248, nel Hohenzollern, Preußen, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Nel 1236, la sua occupazione è indicata come regentschaft als graf von freiburg. Morì il 24 settembre 1271, all'età di 46 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urachが約1226年に生まれた時，父Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburgは43歳，母Adelheid von Neuffenは29歳であった。1248年5月18日にドイツプレウssンホーヘンゾラーンでSophia von Hohenzollernと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。1236年，職業はregentschaft als graf von freiburgと記録されている。彼は1271年9月24日に46歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach(이)가 대략 1226년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Adelheid von Neuffen(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1248년 5월 18일 독일 프레우ss 호헨졸레른에서 Sophia von Hohenzollern(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 1236년, 그의 직업은 regentschaft als graf von freiburg이었다. 그는 1271년 9월 24일 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach nasceu aproximadamente 1226, seu pai, Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Adelheid von Neuffen, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Sophia von Hohenzollern em 18 de maio de 1248, em Hohenzollern, Preußen, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Em 1236, sua profissão aparece como regentschaft als graf von freiburg. Ele faleceu em 24 de setembro de 1271, com 46 anos.

ru.Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendin: эмменденген, эмменденген, крис эмменденген, баден, Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach родился около 1226 г., его отцу по имени Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Adelheid von Neuffen, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Sophia von Hohenzollern 18 мая 1248 г. в местности хохензоллирн, Пруссия, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. В 1236 году он работал по профессии regentschaft als graf von freiburg. Он умер 24 сентября 1271 г. в возрасте 46 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Konrad I. Graf von Freiburg, Graf von Urach nació aproximadamente 1226, su papá, Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Adelheid von Neuffen, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Sophia von Hohenzollern el 18 de mayo de 1248, en Hohenzollern, Preußen, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. En 1236, su ocupación registrada era regentschaft als graf von freiburg. Murió el 24 de septiembre de 1271, a la edad de 46 años.
